mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, equity duration becomes more valuable and discount-rate pressure stays subdued. Under this low-rate, low-inflation setup, S&P 500 should post positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: policy rate at or near effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-rate/low-inflation periods do not outperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor income expectations and weaker consumer demand. If unemployment rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average 20-day return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and lower cyclical earnings momentum. If industrial production is contracting on a three-month basis, the S&P 500 should fall over the next 60 trading days because cyclical sectors and profit expectations soften together.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO growth does not predict below-baseline 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or if the sign flips positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST captures stronger consumer demand and housing-linked wealth effects. When retail sales and housing starts both expand versus their recent trend, S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days as revenue expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST strength fails to outperform single-series signals and produces negative 20-day SPX returns in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market is in a restrictive discount-rate regime and future cash flows are priced more harshly. In that regime, any additional upside in CPIAUCSL strengthens the negative equity effect, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate inflation episodes do not produce lower 5-day SPX returns than the unconditional distribution, or if the effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in PAYEMS indicates payroll growth and improving household income, which supports earnings breadth and risk appetite. When payrolls rise for three consecutive months, S&P 500 should deliver positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll-growth episodes do not beat baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not positive across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous downturn in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies a broad demand contraction rather than a sector-specific slowdown. In that case, S&P 500 should weaken over the next 20 trading days because aggregate revenue expectations and risk sentiment both deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-sales weakness does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is no better than either series alone over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A weak housing and production mix, defined by HOUST falling while INDPRO also declines, signals tightening real activity and lower cyclical earnings. Under this supply-side slowdown, S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined housing-production declines fail to forecast negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in more than 40% of triggered cases.
